The WSJ Dollar Index, which measures the greenback against a basket of 16 currencies, is slipping for the third straight day today, sending it falling just below where it was on Feb. 27.  It’s a stunning development for an asset historically viewed as haven currency—a place for investors to shelter during conflict and uncertainty.

items.headline: US defense bill proposes examination of Apple display supplier
items.summary: SAN FRANCISCO (Reuters) -A measure added into a massive U.S. defense spending bill in recent weeks will, if passed, ask the Pentagon to determine whether one of Apple's display suppliers should be listed as a Chinese military company.  Being on the list does not block companies from doing business in the U.S. but will in coming years block them from being part of the U.S. military's supply chain.

items.headline: China's solar giants quietly shed a third of their workforces last year
items.summary: BEIJING, August 1 (Reuters) -China's biggest solar firms shed nearly one-third of their workforces last year, company filings show, as one of the industries hand-picked by Beijing to drive economic growth grapples with falling prices and steep losses. items.headline: Euro Zone Growth Exceeds Expectations: ETFs in Focus
items.summary: EZU slipped 0.6% past month despite stronger-than-expected Eurozone GDP growth and easing trade uncertainty. Growth in Spain, France and Ireland offset declines in Germany and Italy.

items.headline: Exclusive-China polysilicon firms plan $7 billion fund to shut a third of industry capacity
items.summary: BEIJING (Reuters) -Chinese producers of polysilicon, a building block for solar panels, are in talks to create a 50 billion yuan ($7 billion) fund to acquire and shut down roughly a third of production capacity and restructure part of the loss-making sector, GCL Technology Holdings said. items.headline: STMicroelectronics' Restructuring Fuels Loss, Eyes Strong Q3 Recovery
items.summary: STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) reported its second-quarter 2025 results on Thursday, reflecting a mixed performance in a dynamic semiconductor market. While the company’s net revenues surpassed analyst expectations, profitability took a hit due to ongoing strategic restructuring and broader industry headwinds.
